Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default And Default Judgment And Request To Set Aside And Vacate
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ANDREW M. BAUGH OF THE BEDING & WEILL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-925-838-2090 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTINE GARSKE & ATTY. DAVID V. PIPAL OF THE VAN DE POEL, LEVY, ALLEN & ARNEL, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-925-934-6102 APPEAING FOR THE DEFENDANT THE SIDING SPECIALISTS INC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant The Siding Specialists, Inc.'s motion for relief from default and default judgment is granted. Siding Specialists has presented sufficient evidence establishing extrinsic mistake warranting the relief it seeks. Case law establishes that "extrinsic mistake" is a broad concept covering circumstances extrinsic to the litigation that resulted in a party not being able to oppose the case on its merits. (Weil and Brown book at 5:435, 438 and 549-542). The court credits the evidence that Siding Specialists genuinely, but mistakenly, believed that, as a result of the arbitration, it would be dismissed from this case and did not need to take any action beyond what it did. Siding Specialists has satisfied all three requirements for the relief it seeks: a meritorious defense, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense, and diligence. Under these circumstances, equity favors setting aside the default and default judgment. Plaintiff Candlestick View Homeowners Association's assertion that it would be prejudiced by the granting of this motion fails to take into consideration the results of the arbitration and thus is an insufficient basis to deny this motion. Siding Specialists' request for attorney's fees, costs and/or penalties is denied. There has been an insufficient showing to warrant imposition of any monetary sanctions against the Association. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT A&B ROOFING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The court defers to plaintiff's construction of the prime contract, as set forth in Paragraph 17 of the Second Amended Complaint. See Aragon-Haas v. FamilySecurity Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"). Art. 27 of the prime contract indicates that the plaintiff could not compel the subcontractors to arbitrate. Accordingly, defendant has not shown that its negligence was something litigable in the prior arbitration and res judicata applies. In Thibodeau v. Crum (1992) Cal.App.4th 749, 761, the court expressly found that the subcontract was subject to the prime contract and all of the parties were obliged to arbitrate. It is premature for the court to make that finding in this case. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)
